title: Stenocarpus umbellifer
intro: Stenocarpus umbellifer, commonly known as the ironwood, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive umbrella-shaped flower clusters and hard, dense wood. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, while modern research has identified potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Stenocarpus umbellifer include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stenocarpus umbellifer is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Stenocarpus umbellifer is classified within the family Proteaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the protea family. Stenocarpus umbellifer is native to eastern Australia, where it is often found in subtropical and temperate rainforests. The species is known for its distinctive clusters of yellow flowers and is a popular ornamental plant. As a member of the Proteaceae family, Stenocarpus umbellifer has a unique combination of characteristics that distinguish it from other plant species.
